purposeDescription: Deep mechanism study of Abraham Lincoln's political rhetoric (1838-1865). Full annotation pipeline applied to the core tier: passages, claims, interpretations, and scores. Provides the primary evidence base for the american-civil-war-union case.
distinctionFromSacrificeLawSubset: The sacrifice-law-workbench also contains Lincoln documents as one of four comparative cases (american-civil-war-union subset of the sacrifice-law-comparative-41 corpus). That subset is used for cross-case comparison under Koenigsberg's Law of Sacrifice and receives no PPW deep annotation. This corpus (lincoln-deep-28) is exclusively for single-case deep analysis of Lincoln metaphor and political rhetoric; it is not a substitute for or duplicate of the SLW Lincoln subset.

corpusTiers.annotationScope: full pipeline: passages → claims → interpretations → scores
passage segmentation only; no claim extraction or scoring

availableArtifactClasses.notes: corpus.json, documents.json, artifact-index.json created in Milestone 15
Draft claim candidates only; not reviewed or promoted

claimStatus.notes: No claims from this corpus have been reviewed or promoted into PPW findings. All claim candidates, once created in Milestone 16 (PPW #145), will carry reviewStatus: draft until passing the PPW claim-promotion gate (Milestone 18, PPW #129).
